objective: Train a small QLoRA adapter with offline hidden-correct-vs-hard-negative candidate preferences and test whether it improves held-out pass@K coverage at matched sample budget.
primary_success_criteria: DPO coverage@K on held-out MBPP exceeds tuned-hot base sampling at the same K beyond the pilot noise floor.
DPO pass@1 proxy does not regress relative to tuned-hot base sampling.
DPO functional diversity does not collapse relative to tuned-hot base sampling.
Shuffled-pair DPO underperforms real hard-negative DPO.
